item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Faster than waiting for it to happen naturally.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Let's assume that it takes 10 seconds on average for the robot to dig a hole, the robot doesn't ever need power or maintenance, and all of the components of the bodies eventually reach the environment in a usable form. It would take about 250 million (Earth) years to release enough nitrogen to raise the atmospheric pressure to half an atmosphere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
have a robot do it?

Besides, terraforming is always slow. And with each discovery, you add ~100 lbs of Earth biological material to Mars.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That probably isn't effective in any reasonable time frame.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What an incredible smell you've discovered! Let's get out of here!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Will note on #4 that "armed guard" doesn't have to mean "guy standing at the gate with a rifle". It can also mean "guy who spends most of his time sitting in the surveillance outpost made to look like a mausoleum or groundskeeper's shed, armed with a concealed pistol and a baton"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
take the shovel to Mars, begin digging holes.

Voila, corpses.

Use the shovel to cover Mars with human corpses as part of a terraforming plan.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for the feedback, I have edited the article accordingly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article, to me, has a LOT of issues.

1) Grammar: 'The blade is composed of iron, measuring 0.5 meters by 0.325 meters, and holds noticeable signs of oxidation. ' - do you mean the blade is showing signs of oxidation, or do you literally mean it's holding signs of oxidation?

2) How is -2 getting hold of -1 if it's in secure storage? Does it teleport there or something?

3) That seems way too fast for the Foundation to consider downgrading, I'd give it at least several months, maybe years.

4) Armed guards really, REALLY stand out in the UK - I feel like if you insist on there being armed guards (which, considering there's no safety risk, it seems their job could be done by regular guards with class C amnesiacs - I'd check the spelling on that xD) there should maybe include some level of cover story, even if it's as vague as 'army test ground' or something.

I'm afraid I'll have to downvote this one for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Foundation probably wouldn't do this, but I would like them to give -2 a non-anomalous shovel to use so that she could reach her children. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you used a thesaurus a few too many times in the first half of the page. Use revealed rather than unveiled, this is supposed to be cold and clinical, not artistic and mysterious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Something about this makes me feel conflicted. More interesting than the old article, but I may have to dwell on this.

No-vote, I only wish I could explain further.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it.
Question, though: The origin of the appearing corpses is unknown, but what happens to them after? Do they disappear / return to where they came from? Or was this ghost leaving a pile of bodies laying around the graveyard every morning?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: -2 seems a little mixed up. It seems to refer to the woman in one place, and the graveyeard in another.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Underrated, in my opinion. Just creepy enough and to-the-point, with a suitably weird incident, to where it works. Could it serve to be longer? Perhaps. It might be more interesting. But there's something to be said for how effective and how sad it can still be in this short form. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, it's not supposed to be funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh, the wailing ghost woman seems tacked on.

If she started out wailing and lamenting, and as the night progressed she got more and more irritated with all these GOD DAMN SKELETONS SERIOUSLY WHO THE FUCK PUT THESE HERE, I would laugh, at least.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to admit, I'm confused. So there is a shovel, if you touch it to the ground the skeletons of the two kids appear under. There is a ghost that tried to dig for them but now has given up? The fact that the ghost has given up seems random and out of place to me. I'd personally prefer if the ghost was somehow still around.

Well… Technically the ghost can't reach her children if random dead bodies keep blocking her progress, this is why she eventually she just gives up digging.

Also in my headcanon Hugo drowned the children and embodied himself into the shovel to fuck with his wife, the reason he owns his own cemetary was because he was a part of MC&D.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to admit, I'm confused. So there is a shovel, if you touch it to the ground the skeletons of the two kids appear under. There is a ghost that tried to dig for them but now has given up? The fact that the ghost has given up seems random and out of place to me. I'd personally prefer if the ghost was somehow still around.

It feels like a test log would be in order. They could dig up the skeletons and touch the ground with the shovel and see if the skeletons disappear or if they are getting multiple copies. If they are the same skeletons, could they put something on one (like jewelry or clothes) and have that move as well?

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty cool. Kind of reminds me of that guy who keeps killing his wife in alternate realities and dumping her bodies in one place, but more in feel than anything else. If this needs anything, it's some story behind Hugo or Amelia or anyone else. Why is there a cemetery dedicated to this dude?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not really feeling it. The story feels a little… tacked on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A miraculous improvement. Good job, Doc.

item.title: AUTHOR POST
item.content:encoded:
This is rewrite of the previous SCP-1747 entitled "The Shovel", written by Terraform, and was self-deleted at -4. The original article can be found here:

+ Show
- Hide

Item #: SCP-1747

Object Class: Safe

Special Containment Procedures: SCP-1747 is to be stored in a 0.5 meter by 0.5 meter by 1.7 meter steel safe. Containment is currently housed within Sector-19. At no time may any personnel open the safe without authorization from one (1) member of Level 3 security clearance. The containment safe of SCP-1747 must be routinely inspected monthly to ensure that the spread of rust is kept to a minimal degree.

Description: SCP-1747 is a square shovel that is 1.5 meters in length from blade to handle. The blade is composed of iron and measures 0.5 meters by 0.325 meters. The handle is unvarnished maple wood and measures 1.0 meter in length. The shovel was secured by the Foundation in ██████, ██, U.S.A. on █/█/19██. Large portions of the blade have been rusted over. Efforts to eliminate said rust have demonstrated that while the rust is removable, it will return to the shovel within 48 hours. Initial test analysis displays trace amounts of human blood present on the blade of the shovel. DNA tests on the blood have rendered inconclusive results. If SCP-1747 is used on any ground normally penetrable by an ordinary shovel, the blade will stop 3/4 of the way through the ground and become obstructed by a solid object.

Upon usage of the shovel, the tool will strike human remains directly below the shovel's point of entry, regardless of what was beneath the surface prior to excavation, or what material is dug into. Origin and method of placement of said bodily remains are currently unknown and under investigation.

Test results have shown that the time the corpses have been deceased range as recent as █ days, to ██ months. When obtained by the Foundation, minimal rust was observed on the blade and the discovered bodies were only recently deceased and commonly in presentable condition.

Test log format:
Rust level:
Procedure:
Results:
Time since death:
Analysis:

Test A - █/█/20██.

Rust level: 10%
Procedure: Shovel is used to penetrate surface of dirt.
Results: Shovel is obstructed when blade is 3/4 into the ground. Discovery of body of human female, age 19.
Time since death: 3 days.
Analysis: Cause of death is cardiac arrest. Female body appears to contain no anomalies. When found, the corpse was in standard burial position. More specifically, it is laid its on back with its hands on top of the stomach. Body is mostly undisturbed given the circumstances of discovery.

Test B - █/█/20██.

Rust level: 60%
Procedure: Shovel is used to penetrate surface of dirt.
Results: Shovel is obstructed when blade is 3/4 into the ground. Discovery of body of human male, age 46.
Time since death: 57 days.
Analysis: Cause of death is fatality involving motor incident. Male body is beginning to decay. Bruises and fractures are evident in the chest cavity and torso. Body is positioned in fetal position that suggests pain during moment of death.

Test C - ██/██/20██.

Rust level: 90%
Procedure: Shovel is used to penetrate surface of dirt.
Results: Shovel is obstructed when blade is 3/4 into the ground. Discovery of body of human female, age 29.
Time since death: ██ weeks.
Analysis: Cause of death is blunt trauma to the head. Body has suffered a blow on the back of the cranium. Condition of the body is substantially disfigured and mutilated. Analysis of the skull shows minute traces of rust on the point of impact. The bodily position of the corpse is arranged as if the corpse was stuffed into a cramped area by an unknown individual.

Thank you to VAElynx, MadCatUSA, LurkD, Jekeled, Logan Armstrong, Silberescher, and Weizhong for all your feedback! :)
